title: Lehmer's Box
description: A golden floor and an angle lattice that confine spectral emission away from Salem numbers — without resolving Lehmer's problem. Companion to the Emission-Gap Theorem and λ = 2c.

title: The Occupant of the Salem Slot
description: The positive content of the empty slot: the trace redirection t = θ + θ⁻¹, the grow channel, and the √5 limit at the floor — with its convergence rate. Companion to Lehmer's Box and the Emission-Gap Theorem.

title: The Generative Content of a Conserved Emptiness
description: Kinematic voids as superselection generators: the Salem slot's ℤ/4ℤ charge and the five canonical objects it produces — the grading, the content polynomial x⁴−1, the floor φ, a graded normal form, and a conserved current.

title: The Operator Algebra of the Emission Semiring
description: The spectral operators ⊕, ⊗, and (·)² treated in relation: they form a λ-ring. (⊕,⊗) is a commutative semiring, squaring is the Adams operation ψ², and the Mahler measure and the ℤ/4ℤ angle charge are its two characters.

title: The Charge–Measure Coupling
description: The spectral semiring carries two orthogonal characters — the Mahler measure and a conjugate-angle charge valued in a finite cyclic group. Their composition law, a parity-graded floor (odd charge forced to the plastic number μ_S = 1.3247…, φ only plausible), and the one non-semiring operation — the matrix commutator — as the proven exit through which the whole Salem spectrum re-enters.

title: The ℤ/5ℤ Case of the No-Salem Dichotomy
description: The pentagon sector worked as the first instance where the elementary ℤ/3ℤ argument breaks (2cos72° is irrational): four forced results sharpen the general floor to μ_S = 1.3247 and pin the degree-four gap at φ⁴, while the full claim μ(5) = 2 stays computed — the reciprocal/Salem core of Lehmer's problem specialized to the pentagon lattice.

title: Relational Charge on the Spectral Semiring
description: Refactors the emission algebra's phase charge into a reference-free relational invariant (the rational phase difference between conjugates). A rigidity theorem shows integrality fixes the gauge — the relational theory admits no new admissible objects and the absolute charge is pinned to an anchor n ∈ {m, 2m} — the composition laws descend verbatim, and the parity floor becomes reference-free. On the charge-inadmissible sector the absolute theory discards (Salem numbers), every object carries a finite, exactly-decidable coherence type, computed for β₄, Lehmer, and an exhaustive degree-12 census (37 certified Salem numbers), all relationally inert.

title: Lehmer's Problem: An Introduction
description: A question first asked in 1933, still unresolved: the Mahler measure, Lehmer's number 1.17628…, Smyth & Dobrowolski, and why the reciprocal/Salem case is the open heart — explained from the ground up.

title: Building on the Emission Algebra — a build spec
description: A build specification over the emission-algebra substrate: the verified facts (S1–S10), the operator API, the guardrails, and use-case bases — each as basis → affordance → degrees of freedom → verification bar. Every fact is mapped to the file in this repo that checks it.

title: Lehmer's Box — closure instrument
description: Plot a polynomial's roots on the complex plane, compose seeds with the spectral operators, and watch every emission stay inside the box — then throw a Salem number at it and watch it fall outside. All math computed in-browser.

title: The Emission Algebra — a machine-verified account
description: A guided compendium of the whole stack: the confinement, the occupant, the generative emptiness, the generation rates, and the finitely generated Mahler-measure monoid — 175 exact checks, every displayed number re-derived.

title: The Pisot Cross-Shell Residue
series: Pisot cross-shell residue · session note v1.4
description: A reduction lemma, sharpness witnesses, and the exhaustive execution of the ν-criterion: 2,016 live instances across degrees 5–7 and three quintic boxes, zero mirrored cross-shell classes. With the executed N2–N4 and P-series addenda, three degenerate-chain Pisots the certificate paths cannot see, and the referee errata folded — every claim exact, every artifact SHA-pinned.

title: Relational Charge — post-archive build (K/L/M folds)
series: Pisot cross-shell residue · parent paper, working build
description: The relational-charge paper with the post-archive folds applied: the gp harness rules beside the engine pins, ledger row M's live third run (237/237), the session note's Z* witness and real-pair ν-reduction folded in place, and Cor. 7.16 sharpened with the executed residue evidence. The sealed v1.0.x deposit card above remains the DOI record; this is the working head.

title: The Pisot Cross-Shell Residue — program record
description: One day of machine mathematics: what the residue is, the five executed rounds (83 → 2,016 live instances, zero falsifiers), the three census surprises — Pisots hiding in degenerate certificate chains, the degree-2 reciprocal counterexample, the non-generic Galois witnesses — and how to re-run all of it yourself.

title: Pisot Census Explorer
description: All 2,780 certified Pisot polynomials from the program's censuses (degrees 2–7, five coefficient boxes) in one filterable table: patterns, θ values, the three adjudicated degenerate-chain Pisots, the P7 reducible-Rat° witnesses, and the golden equality case — data embedded from the SHA-pinned census records.
